questions.question_html: If {tex}\sec \theta = x + \frac{1}{{4x}},x \in R,x \ne 0,{/tex}then the value of {tex}\sec \theta + \tan \theta {/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\sec \theta = x + \frac{1}{{4x}},x \in R,x \ne 0,{/tex} {tex}\sec \theta = x + \frac{1}{{4x}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan \theta = \sqrt {{{\sec }^2}\theta - 1} = \sqrt {{{\left( {x + \frac{1}{{4x}}} \right)}^2} - 1} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \tan \theta = \sqrt {{x^2} + \frac{1}{{{{(4x)}^2}}} + \frac{1}{2} - 1} = \sqrt {{{\left( {x + \frac{1}{{4x}}} \right)}^2}} {/tex} {tex}tan \theta = \left( {x - \frac{1}{{4x}}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\tan \theta = x - \frac{1}{{4x}},x \in R,x \ne 0,{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\sec \theta + \tan \theta = x + \frac{1}{{4x}} + x - \frac{1}{{4x}} = 2x{/tex} When we will take negative value with tan we will get answer as 1/2x

{tex}\cos 2\theta {/tex} is equals to {tex}cos(\theta + \theta ) = cos\theta .cos\theta - sin\theta .sin\theta = {\cos ^2}\theta - {\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{co{s^2}\theta - si{n^2}\theta }}{{co{s^2}\theta + si{n^2}\theta }} = \frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }}{/tex}

questions.explanation: If {tex}\;x + y = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} then (1 + tanx)(1 + tany) {tex} \Rightarrow (1 + \tan x)(1 + \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - x} \right)){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow (1 + \tan x)\left( {1 + \frac{{1 - \tan x}}{{1 + tanx}}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow (1 + \tan x)\left( {\frac{2}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right) = 2{/tex}

questions.question_text: The range of tan -1 x is
questions.explanation: The domain range of tan function is {tex}(- \frac{\pi }{2}\;,\;\frac{\pi }{2}\;){/tex} and R. But, tan function is one-one and onto only when we shrink range to (-1,1). Now tan function which is one and onto has domain {tex}(- \frac{\pi }{2}\;,\;\frac{\pi }{2}\;){/tex} and range (-1,1) Hence, domain and range of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}{/tex} function is (-1,1 ) to {tex}(- \frac{\pi }{2}\;,\;\frac{\pi }{2}\;){/tex} .

{tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos x} \right)\, = {\tan ^{ - 1}}2\left( {\cos ecx} \right){/tex} {tex}\\{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{1 - {{\cos }^2}x}}} \right)\,\, = \,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{2}{{sinx}}} \right)\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right)\,\, = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right)\\{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{2\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}} = \frac{2}{{sinx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cot x = cot(\frac{\pi}{4})\,\,\, \Rightarrow x = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

We know that principle value branch of {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}{/tex} is {tex}[0,\pi]{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{3\pi }}{4} \notin [0,\pi ]{/tex} but {tex}\left( {2\pi - \frac{{5\pi }}{4}} \right) \in [0,\pi ]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \frac{{5\pi }}{4}} \right) = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \left( {2\pi - \frac{{5\pi }}{4}} \right)} \right) = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos \left( {\frac{{3\pi }}{4}} \right)} \right) = \frac{{3\pi }}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_text: If {tex}co{s^{( - 1)}}x + co{s^{( - 1)}}y = 2\pi {/tex} , then the value of {tex}si{n^{( - 1)}}x + si{n^{( - 1)}}y{/tex} is

questions.explanation: {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right)\, + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( y \right)\, = 2\pi {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\pi }{2} - {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right) + \frac{\pi }{2} - {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( y \right)\, = 2\pi ,\,\,\,\,{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right) = \frac{\pi }{2} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( x \right) + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( y \right) = - \pi {/tex}

questions.explanation: let {tex}x = \cos \theta{/tex} {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}(2{x^2} - 1) \Rightarrow {\cos ^{ - 1}}(2{\cos ^2}\theta - 1){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\cos ^{ - 1}}(\cos 2\theta ) = 2\theta = 2{\cos ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} if and only if {tex}0 \leqslant 2\theta \leqslant \pi \Rightarrow 0 \leqslant \theta \leqslant \frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex}0 \leqslant \pi \leqslant 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos 0 \geqslant \cos \theta \geqslant \cos \left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right){/tex} since cosine is decreasing function {tex}\Rightarrow 1 \geqslant x \geqslant 0{/tex} This is true for all real values of x {tex}\in {/tex} [0,1]

questions.question_text: The value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{a}{b}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{a - b}}{{a + b}}} \right){/tex} is {tex}\left( {a,\,\,b > 0} \right){/tex}
questions.question_html: The value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{a}{b}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{a - b}}{{a + b}}} \right){/tex}is {tex}\left( {a,\,\,b > 0} \right){/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{a}{b}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{a - b}}{{a + b}}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\frac{a}{b} - \frac{{a - b}}{{a + b}}}}{{1 + \frac{a}{b}.\frac{{a - b}}{{a + b}}}}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{{a^2} + ab - ab + {b^2}}}{{ab + {b^2} + {a^2} - ab}}} \right) = {\tan ^{ - 1}}(1) = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

questions.question_text: If x > 0, then {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right){/tex} is equal to

questions.explanation: {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{2} - {\tan ^{ - 1}}x = {\cot ^{ - 1}}x\;\forall x \in R{/tex} {tex}\therefore {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right) = {\cot ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x > 0 Since, we know that {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right) = {\cot ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} when x>0

questions.question_text: Which of the following is different from {tex}2{\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} ?

{tex}3{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = {\sin ^{ - 1}}{/tex}{tex}(3x - 4{x^3}){/tex} holds good for all
questions.explanation: Let {tex}sin^{-1}=\theta \Rightarrow \sin{\theta}=x{/tex} Consider, RHS: {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}(3\sin \theta - 4{\sin ^3}\theta ) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}(\sin 3\theta ) = 3\theta {/tex} if and only if {tex}3\theta \in \left[ {\frac{{ - \pi }}{2},\frac{\pi }{2}} \right]{/tex} {tex}=3sin^{-1}\theta{/tex} Here, it is given {tex}3{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = {\sin ^{ - 1}}(3x - 4{x^3}){/tex} i.e, {tex}\frac{{ - \pi }}{2} \leqslant 3\theta \leqslant \frac{\pi }{2} \Rightarrow \frac{{ - \pi }}{6} \leqslant \theta \leqslant \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sin \left( {\frac{{ - \pi }}{6}} \right) \leqslant \sin \theta \leqslant \sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{6}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{ - 1}}{2} \leqslant x \leqslant \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \left| x \right| \leqslant \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question_text: The number of solutions of the equation {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex} is
questions.question_html: The number of solutions of the equation {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex}is
questions.explanation: {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - {{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right) = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{6} + \frac{\pi }{2} = \frac{{2\pi }}{3}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{3} \Rightarrow x = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}{/tex} Hence, there is only one solution

We know that {tex}cos : [0,1] \rightarrow [-1,1]{/tex} is bijective function {tex}\Rightarrow cos^{-1}:[-1,1]\rightarrow [0,1]{/tex} is inverse of cos function. . {tex}\Rightarrow \cos ({\cos ^{ - 1}}x)\,\, = x{/tex} when {tex}x \in [-1,1]{/tex} here, {tex}cos ({\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{7}{{25}})\, = \frac{7}{{25}},\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\frac{7}{{25}}\, \in [ - 1,1\,]{/tex} .

questions.question_text: The solution of the equation {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}(\sqrt 3 x) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} is given by
questions.question_html: The solution of the equation {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}(\sqrt 3 x) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} is given by
questions.explanation: {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}(\sqrt 3 x) + {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\cos ^{ - 1}}(\sqrt 3 x) = \frac{\pi }{2} - {\cos ^{ - 1}}x = ({\sin ^{ - 1}}x){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sqrt 3 x = \cos ({\sin ^{ - 1}}x) = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3{x^2} = 1 - {x^2} \Rightarrow x = \pm \frac{1}{2}{/tex} x= - {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} does not satisfy the given equation. Consider {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}} \right) + \,{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{2\pi }}{3} - \frac{\pi }{3}\, = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} ,which does not satisfy given equation

questions.question_text: The value of cos -1 (-1) - sin -1 (1) is
questions.explanation: Let {tex}{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( { - 1} \right) = A\,\,\, \Rightarrow \cos A = - 1\,\,\,\, \Rightarrow \,\cos A = \cos \pi \,\,\therefore A = \pi {/tex} and {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( 1 \right)\, = B\,\, \Rightarrow \,\,\sin B = 1\,\, \Rightarrow \,\,\sin B = \sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \,B = \left( {\frac{\pi }{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( { - 1} \right) - {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( 1 \right) = \pi - \frac{\pi }{2}\,\, = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} .Which is the required solution.

We have to find: {tex}3{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right) - 4{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right) + 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} Put x = tan {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}3{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }}} \right) - 4{\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }}} \right) + 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\theta }}} \right)\,\, = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} {tex}3{\sin ^{ - 1}}(\sin 2\theta )\, - 4{\cos ^{ - 1}}(\cos 2\theta ) + 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\tan 2\theta } \right)\, = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} {tex}3.2\theta \, - 4.2\theta \, + 2.2\theta \, = \,\frac{\pi }{3}\,\,\,\, \Rightarrow 2\theta = \frac{\pi }{3}\,\,\, \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{\pi }{6}\,\,\,\, \Rightarrow \,\,x = \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{6}} \right)\,\, = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

questions.question_text: If and x + y + z = xyz, then a value of {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}y + {\tan ^{ - 1}}z{/tex} is
questions.question_html: If and x + y + z = xyz, then a value of  {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + {\tan ^{ - 1}}y + {\tan ^{ - 1}}z{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x\, + \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}y\,\, + \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}z{/tex} {tex}= \,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right]\,\, + {\tan ^{ - 1}}z{/tex} {tex}= \,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}} + z}}{{1 - \left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right)z}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{x + y + z - xyz}}{{1 - xy}}}}{{\frac{{1 - xy - xz - yz}}{{1 - xy}}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{xyz - xyz}}{{1 - xy - xz - yz}}} \right]\,\,\,\,\,x\, + y\, + z\, = \,xyz\\{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( 0 \right)\, = \pi {/tex}

questions.question_text: If xy + yz +zx = 1, then value of tan -1 x + tan -1 y + tan -1 z is
questions.question_html: If xy + yz +zx = 1, then value of tan-1x + tan-1y + tan-1z is
questions.explanation: {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x\, + \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}y\,\, + \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}z\,\\\,\, {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right]\,\, + {\tan ^{ - 1}}z{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}} + z}}{{1 - \left( {\frac{{x + y}}{{1 - xy}}} \right)z}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\frac{{x + y + z - xyz}}{{1 - xy}}}}{{\frac{{1 - xy - xz - yz}}{{1 - xy}}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \,\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{x + y + z - xyz}}{{1 - xy - xz - yz}}} \right]\,{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{x + y + z - xyz}}{{1 - 1}}} \right]\,\,\, = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}[as \; xz\, + yz\, + xy\, = 1]{/tex}

questions.explanation: Put sec -1 2 = A, cosec -1 3 = B tan 2 (sec -1 2) + cot 2 (cosec -1 3) = tan 2 A + cot 2 B = sec 2 A - 1 + cosec 2 B - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [sec(sec -1 2)] 2 + [ cosec(cosec -1 3)] 2 - 2 = 4 + 9 -2 = 11
